name: Config and Validation Wiring

tasks.title: Add default task_classes entries to config.Defaults()
tasks.description: Add default task_classes entries to config.Defaults()
tasks.body:
Add a TaskClasses map to the return value of config.Defaults() in internal/config/config.go. Include an entry for EVERY class that has a corresponding prompt file authored in Phase 2.

The full list from the spec (verify each has a prompt file before adding):

Language classes: coding/python, coding/javascript, coding/typescript, coding/java, coding/csharp, coding/go, coding/rust, coding/cpp, coding/c, coding/ruby, coding/php, coding/swift, coding/kotlin, coding/scala, coding/shell, coding/sql, coding/r, coding/lua, coding/elixir, coding/haskell, coding/dart

Framework classes: coding/typescript/react, coding/typescript/vue, coding/typescript/angular, coding/typescript/nextjs, coding/typescript/svelte, coding/javascript/react, coding/javascript/node, coding/python/django, coding/python/fastapi, coding/python/flask, coding/ruby/rails, coding/ruby/sinatra, coding/java/spring, coding/csharp/dotnet, coding/php/laravel, coding/php/symfony, coding/kotlin/android, coding/swift/ios, coding/dart/flutter, coding/elixir/phoenix, coding/rust/actix, coding/rust/tokio

Non-language classes: architecture, research, writing, design, devops, data, security, testing, audit

Each entry needs a ClassDef with a Description field matching the spec tables. The Model field should be omitted (empty) for most classes. The research class should have Model: "light" as shown in the spec example.

The class keys must match the prompt file paths under prompts/classes/ so that ClassRepository.Resolve can find them.

tasks.title: Wire ClassRepository.Validate() into daemon startup
tasks.body:
After the daemon creates the ClassRepository and calls Reload(cfg.TaskClasses) in internal/daemon/daemon.go (currently lines 115-116), call classes.Validate() and log warnings for any class keys whose prompt files are missing.

The validation should:
1. Call classes.Validate() which returns []string of missing class keys
2. If the slice is non-empty, log a warning (not an error) listing the missing keys
3. Do NOT block daemon startup on missing prompts (graceful degradation)

Use the daemon's existing logger. The message should be clear enough that a user can understand which class prompts they need to create (e.g., "task classes with missing prompt files: [go, python]").

Also update daemon_test.go to verify that startup succeeds both with and without missing class prompts, and that the warning is logged when prompts are missing.

tasks.title: Add --class validation to task add CLI
tasks.description: Add --class validation to task add CLI
tasks.body:
Update the task add command in cmd/task/add.go to validate the --class flag value against the config's task_classes map.

Currently (line ~60), the --class value is accepted as any string and stored directly on the task. Change this to:
1. Load the config (the command already has access to the app context which includes config)
2. If --class is provided and non-empty, check that the value exists as a key in cfg.TaskClasses
3. If the key is not found, return an error listing the valid class values (from ClassRepository.List() or sorted keys of cfg.TaskClasses)
4. The error message should be helpful: "unknown task class 'foo'; valid classes: [audit, coding/go, coding/python, ...]"

Also add tests in cmd/task/add_test.go (or the appropriate test file) that verify:
- Valid class values are accepted
- Invalid class values are rejected with a helpful error
- Empty/omitted --class continues to work (no validation when not provided)

tasks.title: Auto-assign audit class at claim time
tasks.description: Auto-assign audit class at claim time
tasks.body:
Update the TaskClaim function in internal/state/mutations.go to auto-assign Class "audit" to tasks that have IsAudit == true and an empty Class field.

Current TaskClaim (mutations.go) transitions a task from not_started to in_progress. Add logic:
1. After the state transition succeeds, check if task.IsAudit is true and task.Class is empty
2. If both conditions hold, set task.Class = "audit"
3. The IsAudit field remains the authoritative marker for audit identity; Class is purely for prompt routing

This ensures audit tasks automatically get the audit behavioral prompt without requiring --class audit on every audit task creation.

Add tests in mutations_test.go (or the appropriate test file) verifying:
- Audit task with empty class gets Class = "audit" after claim
- Audit task with existing class is NOT overwritten
- Non-audit task class is not modified by claim

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Config and Validation Wiring is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify config.Defaults() TaskClasses map keys match prompt file paths under prompts/classes/
Verify task add --class rejects unknown values and audit tasks get auto-classified at claim

breadcrumbs.text: Added 52 TaskClasses entries to config.Defaults(): 21 language, 22 framework, 9 non-language. All keys verified against prompt files. research class uses Model: light per spec.
Wired ClassRepository.Validate() into daemon.New(): prints warning for missing class prompt files, does not block startup. Two tests added.

task-0001.objective: Populate config.Defaults() with TaskClasses entries for every class that has a prompt file
task-0001.what_happened: Added a TaskClasses map to Defaults() in internal/config/config.go with 52 entries: 21 language classes, 22 framework classes, and 9 non-language classes. Every key was verified against the prompt files in internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/. Descriptions match the spec tables verbatim. Only the research class has Model set (to 'light'), per the spec.
went_well: One-to-one correspondence between prompt files and config entries verified before writing
Full test suite passes with zero changes to tests, confirming the new defaults integrate cleanly
improvements: The map is large (52 entries). If the class list grows much further, generating it from the prompt directory at build time might be worth considering
action_items: Validation wiring (task-0002+) should ensure these keys survive round-trip through JSON merge and that unknown-field detection still ignores task_classes values

task-0002.objective: Wire ClassRepository.Validate() into daemon startup so missing prompt files produce a warning without blocking
task-0002.what_happened: Added a three-line Validate() call right after classes.Reload() in daemon.New(). If any class keys lack prompt files, output.PrintHuman prints a sorted list. No structured log call because the logger has no active iteration file at construction time. Added two tests: one with missing prompts (verifies startup succeeds), one with all prompts present.
went_well: Minimal change; the entire feature is three lines of production code
Both new tests pass, full daemon suite still green
improvements: Structured logging (to the iteration log file) would be nice but requires an active iteration. Could add a startup log event in Run() if needed later.
action_items: If structured log queries need to find missing-prompt warnings, add a log event in Run() after the first StartIterationWithPrefix call
